articles.title: OpenAI limits its latest ChatGPT product to Trump-approved customers during cybersecurity review
articles.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI said Friday it is restricting the release of its new artificial intelligence model at the request of President Donald Trump’s administration, the latest in an unprecedented government vetting of AI products for cybersecurity risks.
articles.content:
By MATT O’BRIEN

articles.title: Farmland's Steady Returns May Be Less Steady Than They Look
articles.description: Pensions buy farmland for its calm. Those returns are smoothed by how rarely the land is priced, and sharper AI valuation tools are closing in.
articles.content: American farmland barely moved in the first quarter of 2026. The main institutional index of US row-crop land finished up a fraction over the prior year, the kind of placid result that has made farmland a favorite of pension funds looking for calm. C... [8964 chars]

articles.title: Anthropic Moves Toward Deal With US to Lift Curbs on AI Models
articles.description: Anthropic PBC and the Trump administration are moving closer to an agreement that would lift US restrictions on the company’s top two artificial intelligence models after weeks of talks between the two sides over security of the systems, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Reality Cheque: Big $ for Select Startups
articles.description: Indias seed-and early-stage startups are raising almost twice as much per round as they did a year ago, with venture firms frontloading capital for more mature products and business models that require upfront spending, particularly in artificial intelligence, deeptech, infrastructure and healthtech.

articles.title: Future Promises His New Album "The Real Me" Will Not Feature AI
articles.description: Future recently dropped his first single for his new album "The Real Me," and "Radio" is leading the charge for a very hyped release.

articles.title: US stocks recover from tech route as oil slides on Hormuz shipping recovery
articles.description: Stock markets started the day on the back foot as tech stocks came under renewed pressure after Apple announced price hikes on laptops, tablets and other products, citing spiralling memory and storage costs sparked by the rise of artificial intelligence.
articles.content:
Wall Street's main stock indices overcame an early bout of tech jitters Friday thanks to buy-the-dip traders, finishing mildly lower, while oil prices fell as shipping traffic resumed through the Strait of Hormuz.
